root_causes.cause: R1 has an inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 (R1_IN) that explicitly denies ICMP from the 172.16.0.0/24 network; this will drop ICMP echo-replies from 172.16.0.11 back to 192.168.0.11, causing the observed 0/25 ping replies.
evidence: R1 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains ip access-group in R1_IN (access-group applied inbound).
R1 access_lists (R1_IN) entry sequence 10: action = deny, protocol = icmp, ipv4-address = 172.16.0.0, mask = 0.0.0.255, dst-eq-port2 = echo (shows explicit deny of ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24).
Alert: 25 pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 with 0 replies (indicates echo requests likely forwarded but replies were dropped).

OSPF adjacency between R1 and R2 is full on Serial0/1/0 (R1 sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2), indicating routing between R1 and R2 is up and pointing to an ACL-related drop rather than a routing/adjacency failure.

root_causes.cause: R1 has an inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 (R1_IN) that explicitly denies ICMP from the 172.16.0.0/24 network; this will drop ICMP echo-replies from 172.16.0.11 back to 192.168.0.11, causing the observed 0/25 ping replies.
evidence: R1 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains ip access-group in R1_IN (access-group applied inbound).
R1 access_lists (R1_IN) entry sequence 10: action = deny, protocol = icmp, ipv4-address = 172.16.0.0, mask = 0.0.0.255, dst-eq-port2 = echo (shows explicit deny of ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24).
Alert: 25 pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 with 0 replies (indicates echo requests likely forwarded but replies were dropped).

OSPF adjacency between R1 and R2 is full on Serial0/1/0 (R1 sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2), indicating routing between R1 and R2 is up and pointing to an ACL-related drop rather than a routing/adjacency failure.

data.intent_description: ICMP reachability failure: 25 pings sent from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 with 0 successful replies, causing loss of connectivity to 172.16.0.11. Desired outcome: restore IP reachability from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11.

plan.problem: Inbound ACL R1_IN applied on router1 Serial0/1/0 explicitly denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, dropping echo-replies from 172.16.0.11 and causing 0/25 ping replies.
plan.fix_summary: Insert a permit ICMP rule into ACL R1_IN on router1 (Serial0/1/0) before the explicit deny so echo-replies from 172.16.0.11 to 192.168.0.11 are allowed.

evidence: R1 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains ip access-group in R1_IN (access-group applied inbound).

Alert: 25 pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 with 0 replies (indicates echo requests likely forwarded but replies were dropped).

OSPF adjacency between R1 and R2 is full on Serial0/1/0 (R1 sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2), indicating routing between R1 and R2 is up and pointing to an ACL-related drop rather than a routing/adjacency failure.

evidence: R1 Serial0/1/0 has inbound access-group R1_IN (running-config shows Serial0/1/0 ip access-group in R1_IN).

R1_IN ACL entries (sequence order): seq 5: permit icmp any any; seq 10: deny icmp 172.16.0.0/255.255.255.0 eq echo; seq 20: permit ip any any — the permit icmp any any at sequence 5 appears before the explicit deny, so ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 will be allowed by the earlier permit.
ACL R1_IN is applied inbound on the interface linking R1 to R2 (Serial0/1/0), so the effective ACL on that interface contains the permitting entry prior to the deny.

messages.content:
What was observed
- 25 ICMP pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 returned 0/25 (no replies).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R1 had an inbound ACL (R1_IN) applied on Serial0/1/0 that explicitly denied ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, dropping echo-replies and causing the 0/25 result. OSPF adjacency between R1 and R2 was healthy, indicating the problem was ACL-related.

Changes made
- On router R1, inserted a permit ICMP ACE into ACL R1_IN before the explicit deny (sequence 5). The new ACE permits ICMP so replies from 172.16.0.11 can return to 192.168.0.11.

Verification
- Passed. Running config shows R1 Serial0/1/0 still has ip access-group R1_IN inbound, and R1_IN now contains the permit ICMP entry before the deny, so ICMP is allowed. No remaining issues detected.

Changes made

Verification
